desc:
This chamber hosts a thriving population of varied fungi. Bulbous toadstools
crowd in patches on the floor and phosphorescent mosses shimmer along the smooth
rock walls. Hidden beneath the proliferation of fungi are indistinct piles of
rot in many forms of unclear vegetal or animal origin. A narrow, twisting
passageway exits to the west.
name: Fungal Profusion

desc:
Piled on either side of a central walkway are stacks and stacks of broken or old
weapons, armour, goblin clothing as well as general refuse. Some of the items
have spilled out onto the path, making fast travel here dangerous. The footpath
leads from the west to a rough-hewn stairway leading down.
name: Scrap Heap

desc:
A swiftly, sweeping icy stream courses from south to north here, filling half
the chamber with a chilly, dark liquid. The strange thing is how silent the
river is flowing. To the east, a black eddy swirls into a deep pool.
name: Dark Stream

desc:
Curving northward as the tunnel continues from the west, the darkness seems to
swell thickest in that direction. Downward, the jagged floor begins to descend
rapidly into a veil of shadow, beyond which even the strongest light seems
unable to touch.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The jagged stone floor gradually becomes more level here, from where it sloped
down unevenly from the passage above. Toward the east, the darkness swells
thickest, indicating the tunnel advances in that direction. Faint sounds of
dripping water can be heard echoing strange patterns across the walls,
occasionally mixed with other strange, unfamiliar noises.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
Here the cold stone tunnel, with jagged teeth of rock overhead and along the
walls, meets a sudden dead-end. Strewn about the floor are pieces of broken bone
mixed with half-finished and broken tools, crafted from the surrounding hard
stone. The only way out is south, where the darkness seems to wait in welcome
for any light to foolishly challenge it.
name: Tunnel End

desc:
The cold, jagged walls of rock that comprise the walls of this tunnel begin to
narrow slightly towards the south, where the tunnel appears to continue on into
the darkness. Towards the east, the tunnel continues its solemn, cold march into
the blackness.
name: Branching Tunnel

desc:
A narrow tunnel opening lies to the north, where the cold, jagged walls of
dark-coloured stone also comprise the surrounding cave-end here. Upon the ground
lie fragments of shale-like rock, scattered throughout piles of discarded pieces
of bone. What appears to have been a failed attempt at making crude weaponry out
of bone and stone now serves as litter upon the ground.
name: Abrupt Dead-End

desc:
Here, the cold stone walls that largely make up these dark, damp tunnels turns
northward, where the darkness seems to swell into an opening; no sounds or faint
glistens of sharp rock can be seen in that direction, as is present to the east
and south.
name: Tunnel Bend

desc:
Sloping upwards, the ground here becomes ever-scattered with broken sheets of
rock, amongst which several small bones are mixed. As the hard, rocky floor
rises, so the space between the jagged walls become smaller, making for uneasy
travels as the elevation rises. The tunnel bends in from the south, where very
little can be seen in the engulfing darkness that seems to thicken with distance
in that direction.
name: Ascending Tunnel Bend

desc:
Rising from below and to the south, the rocky floor, littered with stones and
the random bones of small creatures, levels out and begins to swing eastward.
While the tunnel walls are much more narrow as it follows the descent, they
begin to widen out the further east one travels. Several rocky protrusions
thrust out from the cave walls, threatening any whom pass close to them.
name: Steep Tunnel Bend

desc:
Descending from above, the stone and bone-laden tunnel floor evens out
momentarily before turning westward, where the view yields nothing but the
awaiting darkness. Toward the west the tunnel expands outwards, jagged fingers
of rock protruding from the stone walls await any unaware travellers.
name: Steep Tunnel Bend

desc:
Ascending from below, the stone and bone-laden tunnel floor evens out
momentarily, before splitting toward the north and south where the view yields
nothing but the awaiting darkness. Small droplets of water drip from the damp
ceiling above, forming a small puddle from which runs a tiny stream downwards,
flanking the rocky descent to one side.
name: Tunnel Fork

desc:
The sound of dripping water is ever-present here, where moisture can be seen
pooling upon the ceiling before dripping into the puddle-covered stone floor
below. From these puddles, small streams constantly flow downward where the
walls of rock disappear under a cold, dark waterline.
name: Watery Descent

desc:
Dripping water and bat droppings merge here to coat a treacherous, slippery,
sloping tunnel. The water has no apparent source besides the ceiling above, from
which it slowly accumulates to fall noiselessly on the guano-softened ground.
The tunnel opens up into a broader cavern to the north and steepens further to
the south.
name: Guano-Slickened Slope

desc:
Unbearably cold water entirely fills this passageway. The channel flows slowly
southward, where it broadens into a larger chamber. The flow is fed by several
narrow cracks up and to the north. One of the cracks broadens into an open air
tunnel above.
name: Chilly Channel

desc:
Icy water fills a large chamber floor-to-ceiling. A slow current, entering from
a channel to the north and exiting through several cracks to the south, gives
the water an even chillier and more dangerous aspect, and no creature not born
to the cold could abide here for very long. Bobbing slabs of ice at the surface
of the water reveal that there is open air above.
name: Chilly Channel

desc:
The strong presence of bats is unmistakable as great mounds of guano and its
accompanying odour fill this expansive cavern. Far above, a broad crack in the
mountainside shows their path of ingress and egress. The floor of the cavern is
fairly level, though mounded here and there with waste. The cave narrows to a
tunnel to the south, and a narrow crack leads downward further into the
mountain.
name: Guano-Filled Cavern

desc:
The rock of this chamber is oddly smooth, rising upward like a bowl from a
shallow depression in its centre. A remarkably straight and level tunnel cuts
northward through a door bordered with weathered runes. Mounds of guano pile
beneath a narrow crack in the otherwise polished ceiling, coating a pile of
rubble that rises up to the crack to create an unintended exit from this room.
name: Befouled Chamber

desc:
A steep tunnel descends from the west to join a larger north-south passage.
Regularly spaced steps lead upward, shallow enough to befit the strides of
dwarves. The floor and walls are smooth and polished, yet dusty with disuse.
However, a few footprints of varying apparent age indicate these tunnels have
not been completely abandoned.
name: Branching Tunnel

desc:
A passageway leading in from the south meets a winding stone staircase that
descends steeply northward. The rough, natural ceiling suggests the builders
simply took advantage of a natural opening in the rock. In several places along
the stairs, deep gouges indicate exploratory mines of ore veins that apparently
proved to be a disappointment.
name: Stone Staircase

desc:
While the tunnel runs almost perfectly straight from this point eastwards, its
floor slopes with frequent changes, and even slight reversals of the grade to a
general trend of a westward descent. The walls of this sloped tunnel are made of
natural rock formations.
name: Sloped Tunnel

desc:
There is a sense that this tunnel continues for an eternity in both the east and
west directions. The walls are made of natural rock with some thin fractures
separating the geologic formation now and then. The floor is covered with layers
of dust, concealing shards of crushed rock.
name: Dusty Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel extends to the west, nearly ten feet high and widest in the middle,
before terminating at two massive bronze doors that have fallen. A gaping hole
can be seen where the gates used to sit. The northern side of the tunnel ends in
another two massive bronze gates that have been set into the wall of smooth
polished white rock.
name: Broken Double Gates

desc:
A large hallway artificially created by master craftsmen extends to the south
toward a wall. The northern side of the hallway is caved in and rubble now
blocks it completely. To the eastern side of the hallway there is the entrance
to what seems to be a larger hall.
name: Caved-in Hallway

desc:
A long, steep staircase zigzags downward along the floor of a natural opening in
the rock, following the slanting cavern as it bends eastward at the bottom. The
cadence of the steps appears best suited for dwarf legs, though the clearance
above the staircase would admit a much larger creature. Broken picks and shovels
rest inside shallow delvings along the northern wall, none of which seems to
have been particularly fruitful.
name: Stone Staircase

desc:
A long stone staircase zigzags its way along the natural contours of a vast
cavern descending from southwest to the north. The stairs consequently both rise
and fall with steep undulations in the cavern floor. While the top of the cavern
is in most places no more than twenty feet above the floor, several great gaps
extend to distances unfathomable high above.
name: Stone Staircase

desc:
Many stone tables fill a large, square feasting hall delved into the rock.
Though all is covered in dust, a great degree of care and art is evident in its
construction, from intricate bas reliefs of dwarf feasts and battles along the
walls to elaborate patterns carved into the tables themselves. To the north, a
passageway descends through the ruins of a stone gate. A long, winding staircase
ascends into a vast cavern to the south.
name: Ornate Hall

desc:
The ruined remnants of a forge line the eastern wall of this large, square
chamber. The bottom half of a massive broken cogwheel rests in a long, narrow
sluice that vanishes beneath a pile of rubble to the north. The shattered pieces
of the forge itself would be scarcely recognisable except for the surprisingly
intact bellows nearby. The only anvils that remain are broken beyond repair. A
vast arcade extends westward from this chamber, and an ornate stone stairway
runs upward and to the south.
name: Shattered Forge

desc:
Water drips through numerous cracks in the ceilings and walls of this chamber,
forming runnels that merge to flow down a set of steps leading to an underground
river below. Stalactites in plenty hang from the ceiling, dripping frequently to
add their contributions to the stream. A squared passage rises upward to the
south.
name: Watery Descent

desc:
An arched hall of magnificently carved obsidian ends abruptly at a great
collapse along the western wall. Fine debris spreads away from the rubble,
covering the broad, meticulously smooth floor. Efforts to break through the
rubble appear to have ended in failure, a story told by crushed remains of both
dwarves and goblins.
name: Collapsed Arcade

desc:
Emerging from a grey rockface to the south through a dark opening worn smooth by
untold aeons of wear, a swift and icy river flows through the wide, open tunnel.
The strong current carries from south to north in a silent race to unknown
destinations. A rough stairway has been cut into the bare slate on the eastern
rockwall.
name: Subterranean River

desc:
Dark, grey waters swirl and eddy here, fed by the current to the west. A gentle
suction pulls the dark water downward in an unstable spiral to an underground
chamber from which a faint glimmer can be seen. Along the edge of the eddy, a
crude path has been chiselled, winding upward to a dark room.
name: Swirling Pool

desc:
The chilly and murky water in this chamber makes it difficult to see more than a
few feet in any direction. Coating the floor is a thick layer of soft, dark
brown sediment. Occasionally, a glimmer shines through the murk.
name: Submerged Chamber

desc:
Shadows draping from the enormous pillars in this room hint at the former
grandeur before the northern and western side partly collapsed. Now this part of
the room is covered with rubble and the walls are partly covered in mould. In
the centre of the room, stairs vanish into the floor leading deeper underground.
name: Partly Collapsed Room

desc:
A narrow stairway made of simple stone descends deeper under the ground toward
the east. The stairs and walls are covered with small cracks. Some chunks of
rock and smaller pebbles make it hard to actually move on the stairs.
name: Narrow Stairway

desc:
The floor and walls of this chamber are made of polished stone. On the west side
of the chamber there are quite some chunks of rocks and rubble before a narrow
stairway that leads out of the chamber. The walls have some cracks which are
also the source of some moisture making it possible for mould to grow and cover
most of the room.
name: Wet Chamber

desc:
The entire tomb is covered in slime and mould, growing especially thick on the
wet walls. A sarcophagus stands on a small pedestal in the centre of the room.
Beside the sarcophagus a mould covered statue of a female scholar rests in a
thinking pose.
name: Mould Covered Tomb

desc:
A large sarcophagus made of stone is standing on the floor in the middle of the
room. Behind the sarcophagus toward the wall stands a statue of a male dwarven
priest. The walls and ceiling behind the statue are covered with smaller cracks
and the floor is overcast with rock pebbles.
name: Crumbling Tomb

desc:
At the northern side of this alcove there is the bottom end of a large and steep
chute which seems to be impossible to ascend. The alcove itself is unadorned and
seems to have the practical purpose to storage materials that are transported
with the chute. The western side of the alcove is covered with fungi.
name: Bottom of a Chute

desc:
A large hallway artificially created by master craftsmen extends to the north.
The southern side of the hallway is caved in from the western side and the
hallway is now blocked by earth and rubble. Between the rubble some water seems
to be flowing out of the western wall into a small natural basin. Everything
around that basin is covered by mould including the eastern wall.
name: Mould Covered Hallway

desc:
A divergence in the masterfully crafted hallway ascends to a very large stone
hall to the east. The hallway continues from south to north while the northern
side features a simple mechanism to block off the hallway with a heavy slab.
name: Divergence in a Hallway

desc:
This astonishing stone hall features marble floors and stunning ornamentation.
This room is at least a hundred feet high and the most distinct feature is a
huge dwarven statue standing next to the eastern wall. Between the statue's legs
sits an entrance to what might be a tomb. A wooden door is set within the stone
wall to the north, and a chute descends rapidly to the south.
name: Stone Hall

desc:
Towering above is a huge dwarven statue, its legs to the south and to the north
forming a gangway. The gangway is made of simple yet beautiful black stone
leading from the stone hall to the west to a stairway east, which seems to lead
deeper under the ground.
name: Gangway into a Tomb

desc:
A large hallway created by master craftsmen extends from south to north. The
floor is laid out with ornate stone tiles, and the walls are made of smooth
polished rock. Toward the southern side there is a mechanism to block off the
hallway with a huge slab.
name: Long Hallway

desc:
A black stone statue of a female dwarven overseer with a hammer in hands stands
infront of a sarcophagus. The stone walls are decorated with small mosaics made
of different metals giving the alcove a very elegant design. The only visible
exit out of this alcove is to the north.
name: Southern Alcove

desc:
Behind a wooden door there is a small room which seems to be a storage. Although
the room is mostly empty now, it was probably used to store materials and tools
a very long time ago. Now the only thing left here is a lot of dust on the
floor.
name: Storage Room

desc:
A small alcove has been skilfully carved into the stone. A decorated fountain
made of quartz stone stands at the southern side of the alcove. Above the
fountain the face of a dwarf is chiselled into the stone. The mouth of the stone
face has a little hole which seems to have been the source of water for the
fountain which is now totally dry.
name: Small Alcove

desc:
The staircase, made of polished marble stone, spirals deeper under the ground.
The colour of the walls and stairs change between white and black while a single
line of golden ornamentation helps with the general sense of direction.
name: Plain Stairway

desc:
The floor and walls of this tomb are made of marble stone with thin golden
ornaments. The most distinct feature of the room are the colours of the floor
and walls which are black to the northern side and white to the south. In the
centre of the walls there are entrances leading deeper into the tomb.
name: Vestibule in a Tomb

desc:
A large sarcophagus made of black metal is standing on the floor in the middle
of the room. Behind the sarcophagus toward the wall of the alcove stands a black
statue of a male dwarven lord. The walls behind the statue are covered with thin
golden ornaments giving the room a very elegant feeling.
name: Black Tomb

desc:
A sarcophagus made of silvery white metal is standing on the floor at the back
of the room. The white marble statue of a fierce dwarven lady stands in the
centre of the tomb, arms crossed. The walls behind the statue are neatly
embellished with golden ornaments.
name: White Tomb

desc:
This large cavern branches off to the north, south, and east. The stone floor
shows the wear and tear of many goblin claws. On the western wall is inset the
goblin equivalent of a bulletin board. Presumably, it is used to post edicts
from the Chief, patrol duties, shift changes and the latest enemies killed - of
which there are none at the moment.
name: Goblin Junction

desc:
Crude chiselling and chipping have resulted in jagged and craggy walls framing
this passageway. The only smooth thing here is the hard stone floor showing the
wear of much traffic over many years. Passages lead north, south, and west.
name: Crude Passageway

desc:
Several heads, most of them old and little more than bones, adorn the walls of
this long, low-ceilinged tunnel. Several torches, long burnt out, are mounted
alongside the skulls and must have given a ghastly atmosphere to the tunnel when
lit. Most of the heads are human or display the nasty fangs of orc or goblin.
name: Tunnel of Heads

desc:
The western wall of this cave is one of many goblin worksites scattered
throughout the caverns. Recent excavations show where the goblins have dug for
ore or gemstones. The walls are shored up by uneven, roughly hewn timber that
looks anything but stable.
name: Goblin Worksite

desc:
The passageway bends from north to east with a ceiling low enough to make a cave
troll stoop. Running through the dark, craggy stone of the walls are veins of
glossy, black rock with corners and edges deadly sharp to the touch.
name: Gloomy Bend

desc:
In this well-lit and spacious cavern, a huge wooden desk dominates with
authority. Functioning as a guardpost before a wide stairwell set in the eastern
wall, no one gets by without the guards say so. On either side of the stairway
are leering goblin heads of stone, carved to instil fear in the lowly. A couple
of lanterns hang on hooks on the wall opposite the desk, their flames steady and
bright.
name: Security Checkpoint

desc:
In the corner, a wide stairway leads down to a well-lit chamber below. Over the
top of the stairwell is a grinning goblin head. Shields and faded wall hangings
intersperse with glowing torches, denoting this area is of some importance. The
hard stone floor shows scuffs and claw marks leading east.
name: Entrance Hall

desc:
This chamber, connected through empty doorways to the west and south, is large
but sparsely furnished. Some simple benches line one wall and an empty table
stands in front of them. A trophy is mounted on the wall next to the southern
doorway, the remains of a very large humanoid head, but barely recognisable as
such.
name: Crude Chamber

desc:
Large flaming torches brightly illuminate this large cavern. Along the walls
hang shields and dusty tapestries in disrepair. A large rectangular table
dominates the space, cluttered with dirty plates, spilled goblets, and other
signs of recent use.
name: Great Hall

desc:
Fading parchments show rough sketches detailing past plans for raids on a
variety of regional targets. The chieftain of this goblin tribe is obviously
very bold and arrogant. A large square table sits here, holding up a large pile
of these papers.
name: Planning Room

desc:
The east wall is covered with several different sets of rusted, worn out body
armour: chain mail, brigandines, and the like in massive sizes. On the south
wall is a large display of weapons ranging from twin-bladed axes to jagged
longswords to all sorts of crushing devices - all in disrepair.
name: Armoury

desc:
Great braziers mounted on four basalt columns light up every part of this throne
hall, bathing it in a deep, orange glow. Intricate designs carved into the domed
ceiling dance in the flickering light, while dark statues look down upon a
crimson rug that splits the entire cave in half from the doors to the throne.
The attempt at grandeur in this chamber bespeaks an arrogance as large as its
owner, the goblin chieftain.
name: Chieftain's Throneroom

desc:
Wide enough for six goblin troopers to march abreast, this long passage has
fairly low ceiling. Dotting the rough, chipped walls on either side are
flickering torches set in rough metal sconces. The well-trodden walkway curves
from the south to the west.
name: Curving Tunnel

desc:
Many pairs of goblin feet have clawed their way to the east and west. The stone
floor has been pounded by all the foot-traffic. No dust along the walkway
indicates this tunnel has been recently used. Dimly flickering torches line the
walls.
name: Trampled Tunnel

desc:
After resting, eating and enjoying some off-duty time in the caves to the west
and south, this is where small troops form up before moving out on patrol. A
large assortment of weapons and other necessary gear hang on the north wall,
dimly lit by several torches along the walls.
name: Assembly Room

desc:
Various sorts of tables and contraptions are laid out here, designed to
entertain and challenge the goblins in their down time. From throwing knives at
a bloody wall target to claw-wrestling, goblins enjoy what little off-duty time
they can get here. Torch-light spilling in from the east casts gloomy shadows
throughout this cavern.
name: Game Room

desc:
A single long, wide table occupies the central area of this part of an expansive
cavern. Various food items, mostly large wooden plates with meats occupy the
table. Several large mugs stand on the table, or lie on their sides, spilled
contents staining a table that's seen better days.
name: Food Dump

desc:
Though not known for cleanliness, even goblins understand that fouling the
caverns make them unlivable. In the walls and along the baselines, are a series
of small openings and pits for relieving oneself. The stench emanating from
these areas is overwhelming and intense.
name: Goblin Latrine

desc:
A vast glacier stretches down from a mountain to the north to end at a chill,
clear lake. Water trickles into the lake from the glacier snout, and several
large chunks of ice float on the surface. Steep, narrow moraines to either side
offer no purchase for any traveller who would wish to continue up the
mountainside.
name: Glacier Lake

desc:
Parallel ridges run across a broad, U-shaped valley, each evidence of a
glacier's retreat. The ridges are shallow and easy to traverse, and are composed
of soil, gravel, and small rocks. The glacier that abandoned this debris long
ago stands to the north at the edge of an icy lake. The deep valley it carved
bends westward as it descends.
name: Recessional Moraine

desc:
A gently rolling till plain covers the floor of a deep, curved valley with
steeply sloped sides. The pebbles, rocks, and boulders that litter the plain
were abandoned long ago in the rapid retreat of a glacier. A careful eye will
note that a faint pattern of parallel curves can be discerned among the debris,
each following the bend of this valley as it descends from east to south.
name: Ground Moraine

desc:
Two glacial valleys originating higher up in the Misty Mountains merge to form a
broad valley that descends to the east. The meeting of the glaciers that formed
these valleys left a long ridge of debris, as if to attempt to keep apart the
two competing siblings. To the west, the farthest march of the combined glacier
is evident in a tall terminal moraine.
name: Medial Moraine

desc:
Peculiar, teardrop-shaped hills dot a broad glacial valley. The hills are
divided neatly down the middle by a tall ridge of sundry debris marking the
separate paths carved by two distinct glaciers to the south and east. The
steepest slopes of the hills face the path of glacial retreat, whereas their
tails descend gently to the west.
name: Scattered Drumlins

desc:
A tall glacier looms at the head of a U-shaped valley, sloping steeply to where
its snout meets scattered, chill pools amid rocks and debris. Further down the
valley, isolated patches of ice have been abandoned in the glacier's retreat,
doomed eventually to melt. Tall ridges composed of the same glacial detritus
make for impassable ascents to either north or south.
name: Glacier Snout

desc:
A vast glacier looms at the head of a broad valley, its terminus steep and
impossible to climb. Fallen blocks of ice mingle with varied rocky debris
beneath the ice wall. To the north, the glacier valley broadens as it merges
with another. The only passage eastward would follow a shallow ascent up to the
northern rocky ridge built up by the glacier.
name: Terminus of a Glacier

desc:
A flat, narrow ridge overlooks a massive glacier to its south which was
responsible for its creation. This vantage point affords a striking view down
the valley to the west and of the debris that marked the glacier's long-ago
retreat. Narrow crevasses cut across the glacier, none large enough to admit any
curious creature foolish enough to investigate.
name: Lateral Moraine

desc:
A tall ridge running along the northern edge of a glacier meets a crevasse
cutting across the entire valley. The ridge dips where it has eroded to form a
shallow slope that descends to meet the icy crack in the glacier. Rubble and
blocks of ice clutter the swiftly narrowing entrance.
name: Before a Crevasse

desc:
A narrow crevasse cuts through icy walls that extend apparently beyond limit to
heights far above. Numerous cracks run upward along the walls, and long,
precarious-looking icicles extend from jagged points left by fallen ice. The
ground is slick with refrozen meltwater.
name: Narrow Crevasse

desc:
A wide cleft in glacial ice broadens as it runs southward. Here and there, gaps
in the glacier hundreds of feet above reveal glimpses of a sky, and a world
beyond this endless ice. Rubble intermingles with shattered ice blocks to form a
rough but navigable passage.
name: Broad Crevasse

desc:
A tall, rocky ridge cuts across the middle of a broad valley, marking the
farthest conquest of a glacier originating higher up in the mountains to the
east. The ground is covered in small pebbles and sand, making for poor footing.
To the north, the ridge leads up to a narrow ledge that cuts across the steep
mountain slopes, whereas a slide down the unstable far slope of the ridge would
seem to be a one-way trip to a valley cutting southeastward.
name: Terminal Moraine

desc:
A nearly vertical slope curves from the north to the east, sloping away from a
pair of peaks high above. The cliff is broken only by a thin shelf no wider than
the breadth of three men. A faint trail is barely visible in the shadowy depths
of a valley below.
name: Steep Mountainside

desc:
A narrow ledge zigs and zags back and forth along the face of a cliff. Its outer
edge is crumbling, leaving great, jagged gaps that leave only a few feet of
passable terrain between a steep slope and steeper drop-off. Debris from the
ledge, boulders, and scree litters the mountainside below.
name: Jagged Ledge

desc:
Smooth, flat rock juts outward from a mountain peak to the north, coming to a
jagged edge where large slabs of rock have broken off to crumble into boulders
strewn along the slope below. Far below, a trail winds back and forth between
this precipice and a similarly steep set of cliffs on the opposite side. A
narrow, rocky ledge descends eastward along the slope of the mountain.
name: Jagged Precipice

desc:
The chilly waters within this subterranean river are swift and strong. They pull
on everything, carrying it downwards while the rocky ceiling gets progressively
lower until nothing but claustrophobic, murky water exists. The water swirls and
descends through a series of fast bends, painfully striking everything against
the tunnel's rocky walls.
name: Subterranean River
